sourceMovie.title: Antichrist
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Antichrist for the same reason it broke through in 2009: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a horror rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Lars von Trier keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Girl Next Door
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Antichrist clicked for you, The Girl Next Door (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Antichrist?
faq.answer: Try The Girl Next Door, Chained, Shut In—each shares DNA with Antichrist while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Antichrist?
faq.answer: Antichrist (2009) is best described as Drama, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Antichrist worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
